title: podocarpus decipiens
intro: Podocarpus decipiens, also known as the New Caledonian yellow podocarp, is a species of coniferous tree native to New Caledonia in the Pacific. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bark and leaves of the tree are used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, providing health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Podocarpus decipiens are typically boiled or steeped in water to create a tea or infusion for medicinal use.
